title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
question: What are the two complementary verified-code tasks used to evaluate AxDafny?
gold_excerpt: We evaluate AxDafny on two complementary verified-code tasks. DafnyBench measures proof-hint synthesis: the implementation and specification are given, and the agent must restore missing invariants and assertions so that Dafny verifies the file (Loughridge et al., 2024). LCB-Pro-Dafny, introduced in this work, measures program synthesis: the agent receives both a natural-language problem description and Dafny specification, and must generate the implementation and proof artifacts needed for verification.

title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
question: How many problems are included in the LCB-Pro-Dafny benchmark and what is their difficulty distribution?
gold_excerpt: LCB-Pro-Dafny contains 250 LiveCodeBench-Pro (Zheng et al., 2025) competition problems translated into Dafny: 100 easy, 100 medium, and 50 hard instances.

title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
question: What is the verification success rate of AxDafny with Gemini3.1-Pro on DafnyBench?
gold_excerpt: AxDafny with Gemini3.1-Pro verifies 725 / 782 benchmark instances (92.7%)

title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
question: How does AxDafny's performance compare to DafnyPro on DafnyBench?
gold_excerpt: DafnyPro reports 86.2% for its full system, which includes a retrieval component from a manually curated proof-hint library.

title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
question: What does Table 3 report regarding the LCB-Pro leaderboard results?
gold_excerpt: Table 3 reports standard LCB-Pro leaderboard results under the benchmark’s original executable-code evaluation.

title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
question: How do the evaluation measures of LCB-Pro and LCB-Pro-Dafny differ?
gold_excerpt: standard LCB-Pro measures whether generated Python code passes runtime tests, while LCB-Pro-Dafny measures whether generated Dafny code verifies against formal specifications.

title: FlexViT: A Flexible FPGA-based Accelerator for Edge Vision Transformers
question: What toolkit and hardware were used to design and evaluate the FlexViT accelerator?
gold_excerpt: we utilized the SECDA-TFLite toolkit [6] and the PYNQ-Z2 board, which contains an AMD Zynq-7000 edge SoC with an edge FPGA and an ARM Cortex-A9 CPU [20]

title: FlexViT: A Flexible FPGA-based Accelerator for Edge Vision Transformers
question: What is the maximum speedup achieved by FlexViT for the DeiT-T model?
gold_excerpt: This results in significant layer-wise speedups (2 _._ 61 _×_ for ViT-T and 2 _._ 74 _×_ for DeiT-T), driving the highest end-to-end gains (1 _._ 40 _×_ ).

title: FlexViT: A Flexible FPGA-based Accelerator for Edge Vision Transformers
question: What percentage of BRAM, DSPs, and LUTs does FlexViT utilize on the PYNQ-Z2 board?
gold_excerpt: The proposed design consumes 83 _._ 9% of the available BRAM, 71 _._ 8% of the DSPs, and 69 _._ 3% of the LUTs, indicating a dense and efficient mapping to the target device.

title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
question: What is the Explanation Exact Match (EM) percentage achieved by the untrained few-shot baseline across all three tasks?
gold_excerpt: Across all three tasks the untrained few-shot baseline reaches only 14–18% Explanation EM, far below the trained explainer, establishing that self-explanation is not a native capability of _M_ 0.

title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
question: What are the results of the self-explainer model _M_ reg (2) compared to the original model _M_ reg in terms of explanation quality?
gold_excerpt: _M_ reg (2) explaining _M_ reg (orange bar in (b)) is not better than _M_ reg explaining _M_ 0 (orange bar in (a)) — both are 78.5% — meaning that _E_ ( _M_ reg) is not an easier distribution to learn.

title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
question: What was the Pearson correlation coefficient for the model _M_ unreg trained without direct behavioral regularization?
gold_excerpt: We show that the correlation is less salient with Pearson _r_ = 0.53.

title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
question: What were the Pearson correlation coefficients for the base model _M_ 0 when analyzing the correlation between behavior and explanation?
gold_excerpt: _M_ 0 without explanation training has no correlation at all. Per-direction _r_ = 0.195/0.237; combined _r_ = 0.196.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What are the three central questions addressed in the experiments of the World Narrative Model?
gold_excerpt: 1) Can the World Narrative Model achieve more precise and comprehensive controllability over video generation than existing methods? (2) Does the proposed 'physical parameter level control' paradigm genuinely improve the efficiency and experience of professional creators? 3) Does each of the various agentic modules of WNM—scene layout, motion/trajectory, and camera make substantive contributions to the overall controllability?

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What does the experimental results demonstrate about the World Narrative Model compared to current state-of-the-art control paradigms?
gold_excerpt: The experimental results consistently demonstrate that WNM not only outperforms current state-of-the-art control paradigms but also provides a practical, deployable solution that meaningfully reduces trial-and-error iterations and empowers creators to achieve deterministic, high-fidelity results.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What are the three design principles used to construct the test set for the video generation system?
gold_excerpt: Our test set is built upon three design principles: diversity of scene types (indoor and outdoor), diversity of motion patterns (single-person and multi-person interactions), and diversity of camera movements (dolly, pan, follow, boom, arc, orbit, _etc._ )

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: How many samples and scenes does the final test set comprise?
gold_excerpt: The final test set comprises approximately 3 _,_ 000 samples, covering 100 representative scenes, over 20 _,_ 000 action patterns, and 16 distinct camera operation types.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What scoring method is used to evaluate the controllability of WNM compared to competing methods?
gold_excerpt: To quantify this, we adopt pairwise comparison with GSB (Good/Same/Bad) scoring, independently judged by professionals with experience in filmmaking or video generation.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What are the three control dimensions evaluated in the fine-grained GSB scores?
gold_excerpt: we decompose the evaluation into three control dimensions to reveal per-aspect strengths and weaknesses: 1) _Scene control_ : scene consistency (correct object placement and layout) and temporal consistency (3D structural stability and cross-frame coherence in long videos).

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What is the overall GSB score achieved by WNM against the text-only baseline?
gold_excerpt: WNM achieves an overall GSB score of 2 _._ 75 against the textonly baseline (novice mode)

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: How does WNM perform against the omni-reference baseline in terms of GSB score and preference percentage?
gold_excerpt: Against the omni-reference baseline (professional mode), WNM still attains a GSB score of 2 _._ 02, meaning that even when competing against the strongest multi-modal conditioning pipeline currently available, WNM is preferred or tied in 66 _._ 9% of the cases.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What GSB score does WNM achieve in scene control against the text-only baseline?
gold_excerpt: WNM achieves a GSB score of 3 _._ 21 against the text-only baseline and 2 _._ 23 against the omnireference baseline in scene control.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: How does WNM perform in camera control compared to the text-only and omni-reference baselines?
gold_excerpt: WNM achieves GSB scores of 3 _._ 33 and 2 _._ 35 against the text-only and omni-reference baselines in camera control.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What are the primary challenges faced in the 'Indoor boxing ring fight' scenario and how does WNM address them?
gold_excerpt: The challenge lies in maintaining precise skeletal articulation for each character while ensuring their limbs do not inter-penetrate. WNM accurately positions each joint at every frame, keeping the boxers’ gloves aligned with the target trajectories and their bodies at the correct distance.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: How does WNM ensure smooth camera motion in the 'Narrow street chase' scenario?
gold_excerpt: WNM’s trajectory waypoints are automatically placed along the street centerline or exactly specified by the director, and the camera path is synchronized to track the character’s position in a classic follow-shot manner, resulting in a smooth, coherent, cinematic chase sequence following the planned geometry.

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What were the reductions in the number of generation attempts for novice and professional users when using WNM?
gold_excerpt: novices reduce attempts from 18 _._ 6 to 5 _._ 1 ( _−_ 72 _._ 6%) and professionals from 9 _._ 4 to 3 _._ 2 ( _−_ 66 _._ 0%)

title: World Narrative Model for Highly Controllable Video Generation: A Paradigm Shift from Pixel Sampling to Physical World Orchestration
question: What was the total creation time reduction for professional users when using WNM?
gold_excerpt: the 48 _._ 4% reduction in total creation time (from 46 _._ 7 to 24 _._ 1 minutes)

gold_excerpt: Preprocessing took on average 0.5 ms per image, inference 2.8. With all other processes, the average speed is around 4 ms per image, or approx. 250 images per second.

gold_excerpt: Out of the approx. 4.000.000 processed images (out of which 1.507 contained music notation), 570 non-notation images were let through the system, for a combined FPR of 0.015 %.

title: MVP-Nav: Multi-layer Value Map Planner Navigator
question: What are the three indoor object-goal navigation benchmarks used to evaluate MVP-Nav, and how many validation episodes does each benchmark consist of?
gold_excerpt: We evaluate the performance of **MVPNav** across three widely-adopted indoor object-goal navigation benchmarks, namely HM3D [24], MP3D [5], and RoboTHOR [9]. Specifically, HM3D consists of 20 high-fidelity indoor scene reconstructions encompassing 2K validation episodes across 6 target object categories, while MP3D provides 11 real-world indoor environments with 1.8K validation episodes covering 20 categories. Additionally, we utilize RoboTHOR, which includes 1.8K validation episodes across 15 indoor environments involving 12 goal categories.

title: MVP-Nav: Multi-layer Value Map Planner Navigator

title: MNAR-$k$-means: A $k$-means Clustering for Data Missing Not at Random with Magnitude-Decaying Probability
question: What assumption is made regarding the missing probability in the MNAR mechanisms, particularly in relation to low-expression genes?
gold_excerpt: Through this paper, we focus on a common scenario of MNAR mechanisms with magnitude-decaying missing probability, where data is more likely to be missing at positions with smaller absolute values.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: What is the method proposed for improving preference optimization and training data selection in LLMs?
gold_excerpt: We propose to leverage metacognitive feedback to improve preference optimization and training data selection, demonstrating the value of this paradigm by applying it to achieve holistic faithful calibration (FC) of LLMs.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: How does the RL framework operate in terms of evaluating candidate completions?
gold_excerpt: Post-generation, each _rg_ is evaluated using a composite reward function that captures the end goal of faithful alignment between expressed and intrinsic confidence, and key quality dimensions of correctness (to preserve task accuracy), factual calibration (to mitigate the factual-faithful calibration tradeoff [113, 62]), and format adherence.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: What datasets were used to evaluate the FC performance of the metacognitive approach?
gold_excerpt: We evaluate FC performance using a suite of 10 datasets spanning diverse formats, content domains, and difficulty levels.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: Which models were applied in the experiments and what was the training procedure?
gold_excerpt: We apply our approach to LLMs from two widely used model families: Qwen3 (1.7B, 4B, 8B) [109] and Llama3.1-Instruct (8B) [29].

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: What percentage gains in average cMFG does the two-stage approach of RLMF achieve compared to prior prompting and SFT-based methods?
gold_excerpt: our two-stage approach achieves respective gains of **29%** and **25%** in average `cMFG` * across tasks

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: How does RLMF compare to standard RL in terms of improving post-training results and metacognitive monitoring?
gold_excerpt: `RLMF` **outperforms standard** `RL` **to both improve post-training results and endow models with better metacognitive monitoring.**

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: What models were used in the experiments, and what sizes do they come in?
gold_excerpt: In our experiments, we used the following models, varying in size, family, and post-training: Qwen3 (1.7B, 4B, 8B) [109], Llama3.1-Instruct (8B) [29].

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: What were the training parameters and batch sizes used during the GRPO training setup?
gold_excerpt: Per-device training batch size was set to 2, with gradient accumulation of 8 across 4 GPUs, yielding a total effective batch size of 64.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: What is the final reward structure used in the GRPO methodology for faithful calibration?
gold_excerpt: The final reward is a weighted sum of individual rewards, wherein good FC is prioritized via relatively higher weight, and no unfaithfully calibrated output can achieve a higher total reward than a faithfully calibrated one.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: What penalties were explored during the initial iterations of the GRPO pipeline, and what was the outcome?
gold_excerpt: In initial iterations of our pipeline, we explored the value of additionally using a task-specific length penalty during GRPO and foregoing the use of pre-SFT; this length penalty was defined in a binary fashion based on the number of observed sentences in a completion: if the number of sentences did not exceed the permissible maximum sentence count for the training dataset (e.g., 2 sentences for PopQA), a penalty of -1.0 was applied.

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: What were the average `cMFG` scores for the Llama3.1-8B-Instruct model trained with different numbers of hedges per confidence bin?

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: How does the average `cMFG` score of the single-pass rewriting approach compare to the fine-grained approach for both Llama3.1-8B-Ins and Qwen3-8B models?
gold_excerpt:
|Rewriting Approach|Model|Average`cMFG`*|
|---|---|---|
|Single Pass (Ours)|Llama3.1-8B-Ins|**0.82**|
|Single Pass (Ours)|Qwen3-8B|**0.83**|
|Fine-Grained|Llama3.1-8B-Ins|0.80|
|Fine-Grained|Qwen3-8B|0.82|

title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
question: How many expert annotators were involved in the human annotation study, and how many examples did they annotate?
gold_excerpt: We used three expert annotators (graduate students working directly with LLMs) and instructed them to provide preference annotations on 120 examples.

title: SpheRoPE: Zero-Shot Optimization-Free 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE
question: What are the two core topological invariants imposed by ERP that standard diffusion models struggle with?
gold_excerpt: Standard diffusion models struggle with two core topological invariants imposed by ERP: horizontal periodicity (perfect continuity between the left and right edges) and polar convergence (a coordinate singularity where all columns meet at the poles).

title: SpheRoPE: Zero-Shot Optimization-Free 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE
question: How is the mapping of spherical coordinates to pixel coordinates defined in terms of height, width, and channels?
gold_excerpt: Formally, let **x** _∈_ R _H×W ×C_ denote an ERP panoramic image (or video frame) with height _H_ , width _W_ = 2 _H_ , and _C_ channels.

title: SpheRoPE: Zero-Shot Optimization-Free 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE
question: What datasets were used for image and video evaluation in the experiments?
gold_excerpt: For image evaluation, following [52], we use the ODI-SR dataset [9]: 1,200 ERP panoramas spanning diverse indoor and outdoor scenes, captioned with Qwen3-VL [45]. For video evaluation, we use two prompt sets: _SphereDiff-20_ , a 20-scene benchmark from [32], and _Stress-20_ , a set of 20 prompts generated by an LLM (Claude Opus 4.7) to stress-test panoramic generation with high-motion, dynamic content.

title: SpheRoPE: Zero-Shot Optimization-Free 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE
question: How does the performance of the zero-shot approach compare to trained baselines on the ODI-SR dataset?
gold_excerpt: Table 1 shows our zero-shot approach matches or outperforms trained baselines on ODI-SR. At the panorama level, we achieve the best FAED score without task-specific training, demonstrating that our outputs most accurately capture the global distribution.

title: SpheRoPE: Zero-Shot Optimization-Free 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE
question: What resolution is used for generating ERP panoramas in the experiments?
gold_excerpt: we produce ERP panoramas at a resolution of 1024 _×_ 2048 using 50 denoising steps.

title: SpheRoPE: Zero-Shot Optimization-Free 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE
question: Which model and dataset are used for the LLM-based evaluation of 360° panorama generation?
gold_excerpt: we use GPT-4o to score 14 perspective views per scene on a 1–5 scale across panoramic and image criteria, using their 20-prompt benchmark.

title: LeCropFollow: Latent Space Planning for Navigation in Unstructured Crop Fields
question: What is the name of the navigation framework proposed in the paper?
gold_excerpt: **LeCropFollow** , a navigation framework that maps high-dimensional visual observations directly to control inputs without explicit state estimation.

title: LeCropFollow: Latent Space Planning for Navigation in Unstructured Crop Fields
question: How is the perception backbone trained in the LeCropFollow system?
gold_excerpt: the perception backbone (trained via self-supervised learning) and the control policy (trained via model-based reinforcement learning)

title: LeCropFollow: Latent Space Planning for Navigation in Unstructured Crop Fields
question: What robot was used in the experiments and what instruments were it equipped with?
gold_excerpt: Experiments utilized the TerraSentia skid-steer robot (EarthSense Inc.), instrumented with wheel encoders, a 6-DoF IMU, a ZED 2i camera (right monocular stream), and a Livox Mid-360 LiDAR to support the **CROW** [4] baseline.

title: LeCropFollow: Latent Space Planning for Navigation in Unstructured Crop Fields
question: What were the benchmarked systems and what was the target velocity during the experiments?
gold_excerpt: We benchmarked against **CROW** and **CropFollow++** [6] using official public checkpoints, with all systems running at 20 Hz with a target velocity of _v_ = 0 _._ 9 m/s.

title: Dynamic Prediction of Alternating Recurrent Events via Neural Network
question: What metrics are used to evaluate model performance in the testing set?
gold_excerpt: Model performance is evaluated in the testing set using average bias, the C-statistic (also referred to as the index of concordance or Harrell’s C-index) (Harrell et al., 1982) and root error sum of squares.

title: Dynamic Prediction of Alternating Recurrent Events via Neural Network
question: What does a C-statistic value of approximately 0.50 indicate?
gold_excerpt: C-statistics measure predictive discrimination, and a value of approximately 0.50 indicates poor discriminative ability, while perfect prediction yields a C-statistic of 1.

title: Dynamic Prediction of Alternating Recurrent Events via Neural Network
question: What are the performance metrics used to evaluate the dynamic prediction models in the study?
gold_excerpt: Panels A, B, C and D show the average C-statistic, percent bias, RESS results, and percent absolute bias respectively, all in the testing set (data held out and not used in training).

title: Dynamic Prediction of Alternating Recurrent Events via Neural Network
question: How does the performance of the LSTM model compare to the GEE Main Effects model at later time points?
gold_excerpt: At later time points, the long short-term memory network outperforms the GEE Main Effects model in both H ℓ and H h settings in terms of C-statistic and testing RESS.

title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines

title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
question: How many company policy guidelines and NDA contracts were used to evaluate PolicyGuard, and what was the total number of policy–contract decisions verified?
gold_excerpt: We use 95 guidelines from an internal playbook and five NDA contracts (NDA-3, NDA-2, NDA-1, NDA-4, NDA-5), yielding 475 policy–contract decisions verified by the company’s legal team.

title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
question: What are the F1 scores for embedding-based retrieval and LLM-guided retrieval at k=5?
gold_excerpt: Embedding retrieval improves from 27.5–31.3 F1 at _k_ =1 to 36.5–38.4 F1 at _k_ =5. LLM-guided retrieval reaches 39.4–42.5 F1 at _k_ =3 and plateaus thereafter.

title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
question: What is the F1 score of PolicyGuard compared to the best retrieval result?
gold_excerpt: The best retrieval result (42.5 F1) is comparable to the full-contract baseline range (42.2–42.9 F1) and far below PolicyGuard’s 73.7 F1.

title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
question: How many policies were randomly sampled from the company playbook for the evaluation of Claude Cowork?
gold_excerpt: we randomly sampled 40 policies from the company playbook.

title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
question: What is the name of the structured configuration file that compiles the sampled policies for Claude Cowork?
gold_excerpt: compiled all 40 policies into a structured configuration file, legal.local.md.
